V1_one_shot_generation_operability.judgment: Generation and deterministic execution are viable, but yield depends on output budget and filtering, content is stochastic, and rule-only coverage is limited.

V2_repair_agent_operational_validity.judgment: One bounded Repair revision recovered all thirteen reproducible static-valid runtime failures without retry and preserved deterministic runtime contracts.
V2_repair_agent_operational_validity.scope_limit: Runtime recovery for this frozen cohort only; semantic equivalence and detection improvement are not established.

V3_repair_agent_detection_utility.judgment: Repaired-rule utility was mixed, only two repairs survived A1 selection, and A1 versus A0 was negative for Alpha and near-zero positive for Beta.

V4_review_agent_inner_effectiveness.judgment: Seventy-two of 77 attempted Review calls improved inner point F1, with one equal, three regressive, and one invalid outcome; 76 revisions were deterministic executables.
V4_review_agent_inner_effectiveness.scope_limit: The strong label applies to inner-only refinement under the frozen trigger and one-revision budget.

V5_review_agent_outer_transfer.judgment: All 19 selected reviewed rules transferred positively relative to their parent, and A2 improved over A0 for both variants; non-selected revisions still included negative and no-transfer cases.

V6_end_to_end_agentic_aggregator_robustn.judgment: A3 versus A0 was negative for Alpha and positive for Beta, so the complete bounded branch was not robust to the unresolved detector variant.

V7_safety_and_efficiency.judgment: Execution containment and bounded calls were strong, but 14 of 19 selected FP rules were harmful by the frozen overlapping classification and the study consumed 90 calls and 404,399 tokens.
